message:
feat(reactor): add cross-platform create_reactor() factory function

Introduces a convenience factory function that automatically selects the
best available reactor backend for the current platform:

Platform selection order:
- Linux: io_uring (if feature enabled and kernel supports), else epoll
- macOS/BSD: kqueue
- Windows: IOCP

This simplifies reactor creation for callers who don't need fine-grained
control over the backend selection. The function returns Arc<dyn Reactor>
for easy sharing across threads.

Implementation details:
- Uses #[cfg] attributes for conditional compilation per platform
- io_uring gracefully falls back to epoll if creation fails
- Returns io::Result for error propagation
- Adds Arc import for the return type

Also adds a test verifying the factory works and produces an empty reactor
with zero registrations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
